heading: Third party validations
label: 968 Third party validations
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsec. (e), is , , , known as the Security and Accountability For Every Port Act of 2006 or the SAFE Port Act. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 109–347120 Stat. 1884Oct. 13, 2006
amendments: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsec. (e), is , , , known as the Security and Accountability For Every Port Act of 2006 or the SAFE Port Act. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 109–347120 Stat. 1884Oct. 13, 2006

children.heading: Plan
children.label: a Plan
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Commissioner, shall develop a plan to implement a 1-year voluntary pilot program to test and assess the feasibility, costs, and benefits of using third party entities to conduct validations of C–TPAT participants.

children.heading: Consultations
children.label: b Consultations
children.text: Not later than 120 days after , after consulting with private sector stakeholders, including the Commercial Operations Advisory Committee, the Secretary shall submit a report to the appropriate congressional committees on the plan described in subsection (a).October 13, 2006

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the consultations described in subsection (b), the Secretary shall carry out the 1-year pilot program to conduct validations of C–TPAT participants using third party entities described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Authority of the Secretary
children.label: 2 Authority of the Secretary
children.text: The decision to validate a C–TPAT participant is solely within the discretion of the Secretary, or the Secretary’s designee.

children.heading: Certification of third party entities
children.label: d Certification of third party entities

children.text: demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the entity has the ability to perform validations in accordance with standard operating procedures and requirements designated by the Secretary; and

children.label: A
children.text: to perform validations in accordance with such standard operating procedures and requirements (and updates to such procedures and requirements); and

children.label: B
children.text: to maintain liability insurance coverage at policy limits and in accordance with conditions to be established by the Secretary; and
signs an agreement to protect all proprietary information of C–TPAT participants with respect to which the entity will conduct validations.

children.heading: Information for establishing limits of liability insurance
children.label: e Information for establishing limits of liability insurance
children.text: A third party entity seeking a certificate under subsection (d) shall submit to the Secretary necessary information for establishing the limits of liability insurance required to be maintained by the entity under this Act.

children.label: A
children.text: any beneficial interest in or any direct or indirect control over the C–TPAT participant for which the validation services are performed; or

children.label: B
children.text: any other conflict of interest with respect to the C–TPAT participant; and
the C–TPAT participant has entered into a contract with the third party entity under which the C–TPAT participant agrees to pay all costs associated with the validation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall regularly monitor and inspect the operations of a third party entity conducting validations under subsection (c) to ensure that the entity is meeting the minimum standard operating procedures and requirements for the validation of C–TPAT participants established by the Secretary and all other applicable requirements for validation services.

children.label: A
children.text: revoke the entity’s certificate of conformance issued under subsection (d)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: review any validations conducted by the entity.

children.heading: Limitation on authority
children.label: h Limitation on authority
children.text: The Secretary may only grant a C–TPAT validation by a third party entity pursuant to subsection (c) if the C–TPAT participant voluntarily submits to validation by such third party entity.
the results of the pilot program, including the extent to which the pilot program ensured sufficient protection for proprietary commercial information;
the cost and efficiency associated with validations under the pilot program;
the impact of the pilot program on the rate of validations conducted under C–TPAT;
any impact on national security of the pilot program; and
any recommendations by the Secretary based upon the results of the pilot program.
